## Improvements

- **Delivery coaching now scores only your own voice — never the other side's.** With the counterpart playing over speakers, the mic used to pick up their voice and fold their pace and intonation into your live meters, nudges, and baselines. The system-audio stream is now analyzed as an acoustic reference (pitch-matched, octave-tolerant — no reliance on speech-to-text), and mic frames that are really speaker bleed are dropped from every "me" statistic. Headphone setups are unaffected.

- **The retro's measured pace is now mic-only too.** The post-call pace read used to be measured over the saved recording, which in diarized meetings mixes both sides. A whole-session articulation rate is now accumulated live from your own mic and saved with the meeting; the recording is only measured directly when it contains just your mic (and for uploaded files, where there is no mic).

- **The intonation gauge clears promptly.** It used to linger on its last value for up to ~7 seconds after you stopped talking; it now reads "no signal" after 2 seconds of silence.

- **Dead-air nudges understand two-sided silence.** With bleed rejection in place, the dead-air check now also requires the other side to be quiet — the counterpart's monologue no longer counts as dead air.

- **Fixed the delivery panel's meter layout.** The English "Intonation" label overflowed its fixed-width column and ran into the meter bar; the label column now sizes to its text in any language, and the pace/intonation rows stay aligned.

- **Onboarding now introduces voice typing.** A new step explains push-to-talk dictation, shows your current shortcut, and offers the same enable/disable toggle as Settings (including the Accessibility grant on enable).
